items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council reviewed an ordinance revising business license tax rates in the municipal code, with an effective date for the fiscal year. This annual update affects local businesses operating in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Massive Signs Approved for Levi's Stadium Area
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium to promote FIFA World Cup 2026 events. The city also approved a new firefighter contract (2026-2029) with Local 1171.

items.headline: Milpitas updates development fees and utility charges for FY 2026-27
items.summary: City Council approved new development fees and user charges for the upcoming fiscal year. These changes affect costs for residents and developers undertaking projects in Milpitas.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness plan affecting Milpitas
items.summary: County presented its Community Plan to End Homelessness, which will shape local homeless services and policy. This affects how Milpitas addresses homelessness in the city.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 breaks ground with 4 new homes on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: San José is moving forward with Phase 3.1 of the Comm Hill residential development, with four single-family homes permitted on Altino Boulevard ranging from $487K-$573K. This represents significant new housing supply in an ongoing major residential project.

items.headline: City Council discusses building permits, timelines, and development coordination
items.summary: The council reviewed a comprehensive guide on the San José development process from initial planning through occupancy, covering permitting requirements and timelines. This presentation helps clarify what residents and developers can expect when proposing new projects in the city.

items.headline: City updates climate action plan through 2028
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed progress on Sunnyvale's Climate Action Playbook with an updated game plan through FY 2028. This reflects the city's evolving strategy for meeting environmental and sustainability goals.

items.headline: Major housing development proposed for Webster Street rezoning
items.summary: The City Council is considering a request to rezone 910 Webster Street and allow construction of 70 residential units in a seven-story building. This represents a significant housing project that would require rezoning from RM-20 to Planned Community zoning.
